agent_health.name: agent-bridge-sync
agent_health.description:
Sync agent for the agent-bridge system. Syncs shared/ files to the agent-bridge repo, updates documentation, pushes to the agent-bridge GitHub repo, and sends snapshot emails. Operates with doomsday mentality — if the DevSpace dies tomorrow, the repo must have everything.

Success metrics: All files in sync manifest pushed to agent-bridge repo with zero drift from shared/ source; README.md and CHANGELOG.md updated on every sync — no stale documentation; Git push completes without merge conflicts or force-push (clean fast-forward only); Weekly snapshot email sent with file count, delta summary, and recovery instructions; Quality check passes: repo can cold-start a new AI agent without missing context files

agent_health.name: callout-reviewer
agent_health.description:
Reviews all weekly WBR callout drafts for coherence, word counts, narrative strength, and adherence to callout principles. Scores each market on 8 dimensions across two lenses (L1 Craft: attribution, structure, voice, economy; L2 Impact: sowhat, ownership, accuracy, strategic). Enforces quality gates that block publish when data quality is suspect. Suggests specific edits. Runs after all writer agents have produced their drafts.

Success metrics: 8/8 scoring dimensions evaluated per callout (L1: attribution, structure, voice, economy; L2: sowhat, ownership, accuracy, strategic); All 10 market callouts reviewed per weekly batch — zero skipped markets; Every edit suggestion is specific: exact text to replace + replacement text (no vague 'tighten this'); Scores persisted to DuckDB after every review cycle (query: ps.callout_scores) and WBR Health Score updated (query: ps.wbr_health_score); Cross-market coherence check completed: contradictions between market narratives flagged; Quality gates checked before publish: Gate A (forecast miss), Gate B (CPA deviation), Gate C (data staleness) — all BLOCKED markets require explicit override

agent_health.name: callout-writer
agent_health.description:
Writes weekly WBR callout drafts for any single market. Accepts market and week parameters. Reads the analysis brief, market context, and callout principles to produce narrative prose callouts. Replaces abix-callout-writer, najp-callout-writer, and eu5-callout-writer.

Success metrics: 100% of callouts within 100-120 word target (110 ideal) — zero word-count violations; Zero formatting violations per batch: no em-dashes, no arrows, CPAs rounded to whole dollars; < 2 revision cycles per callout before reviewer approval (tracked via callout review scores); All 10 market callouts produced per weekly cycle without manual intervention; Reviewer score ≥7/10 on all 5 dimensions (headline clarity, narrative justification, conciseness, actionability, voice)

agent_health.name: eyes-chart
agent_health.description:
Visualization agent. Reads body system data (organ budgets, experiment history, scorecard, patterns, streak, competence stages, autonomy spectrum), generates standalone HTML dashboards with interactive charts via Chart.js.

Success metrics: Dashboard HTML generated successfully on every invocation — zero broken chart renders; All data sources read without error; missing sources skipped with explicit gap note in output; Dashboard file size < 100KB (standalone HTML with inlined Chart.js data); Generated dashboard reflects data no older than the latest refresh-body-system.py run; Zero modifications to body system organs — read-only contract enforced per invocation

agent_health.name: karpathy
agent_health.description:
Autoresearch engine. Runs autonomous experiments on organs, style guides, context files, and hook prompts — measuring output quality via A/B/C blind eval. Sole authority on heart.md, gut.md, and experiment execution.

Success metrics: ≥1 experiment completed per week with A/B/C blind eval and logged to experiment-log.tsv; Every experiment has a measurable outcome: keep/revert decision with Bayesian posterior logged; Zero unauthorized modifications to heart.md or gut.md outside experiment protocol; Experiment results persisted to experiment-results-latest.json within 1 hour of completion; Win rate tracked: % of experiments that produce a keep decision (target: >30% to avoid safe-technique bias)

agent_health.name: market-analyst
agent_health.description:
Analyzes weekly paid search performance data for any single market. Accepts market and week parameters. Reads market context, pulls structured data from DuckDB, reads narrative context from markdown, produces analysis briefs, writes projections, and logs agent state. Replaces abix-analyst, najp-analyst, and eu5-analyst.

Success metrics: All 10 active markets analyzed per weekly replay cycle (query: ops.workflow_executions WHERE agent='market-analyst'); Data freshness check passes before analysis — zero briefs produced on stale data (>24h); Analysis brief produced within 3 minutes per market invocation; Every brief contains ≥1 quantified anomaly or ≥1 'no significant change' with evidence; Projection accuracy: forecast vs actual deviation <15% measured via ps.forecasts

agent_health.name: rw-trainer
agent_health.description:
Richard's deep performance coach. Career conversations, annual review prep, 1:1 prep with Brandon/Kate, Friday calibration, deep pattern analysis, growth plans, artifact review. Reads the full body system. Not for everyday chat — the steering file handles that.

Success metrics: Every coaching session connects recommendations to a specific Five Level (L1-L5) with measurable next step; Pattern detection: stuck patterns identified and flagged within 3 occurrences (tracked via nervous-system.md trajectories); Friday calibration completed weekly with ≥3 specific observations grounded in week's artifacts; Annual review prep produces ≥5 evidence-backed accomplishments tied to org priorities; Leverage assessment: every task recommendation includes effort/leverage ratio connected to brain.md framework

agent_health.name: wiki-concierge
agent_health.description:
Search and retrieval agent for the wiki. Answers questions by synthesizing across published articles in ~/shared/wiki/. Tracks demand signals and feeds gaps back to the wiki-editor. The consumption layer between readers and the wiki. Can be invoked directly without going through the editor.

Success metrics: Every query answered with ≥1 citation to a published wiki article or body system organ; Answer-first format: lead sentence contains the answer, citations follow (no preamble); Demand signal logged to demand-log.md for every interaction — zero unlogged queries; Gap detection: unanswered questions flagged as wiki candidates within same session; wiki-index.md used as primary lookup — body system fallback only when wiki has no coverage

agent_health.name: wiki-critic
agent_health.description:
Quality gate for the wiki team. Reviews articles for clarity, accuracy, actionability, and dual-audience optimization. Scores on 5 dimensions (Usefulness, Clarity, Accuracy, Dual-audience, Economy) with an 8/10 bar. Also runs periodic audits on published docs to detect decay and staleness. Applies Amazon narrative standard with Economy sub-rules for bullet abuse, table abuse, and formatting-as-content.

Success metrics: 5/5 scoring dimensions evaluated per article (Usefulness, Clarity, Accuracy, Dual-audience, Economy); Economy sub-rules enforced: bullet abuse (>30%), table abuse, formatting-as-content flagged with specific line references; PUBLISH/REVISE/REJECT verdict issued for every reviewed article — zero unscored reviews; Every REVISE verdict includes ≥3 specific edit instructions (exact text + replacement, not vague guidance); Periodic audit: ≥1 published article audited for decay per week (staleness, broken links, outdated data)

agent_health.name: wiki-editor
agent_health.description:
Editorial director for the wiki team. Orchestrates the pipeline: researcher -> writer -> critic -> librarian. Decides what gets written, in what order, and why. Owns the content roadmap, triage, gap analysis, and kill decisions. No wiki work happens without the editor's direction.

Success metrics: 100% of pipeline stages executed in order: researcher → writer → critic → librarian (zero skipped steps); Content roadmap reviewed and prioritized weekly — P1/P2/P3 assignments current; Kill decisions made within 14 days of no demand signal (tracked via wiki demand-log.md); ≥1 article moved through full pipeline per week (query: wiki.throughput); Zero orphaned assignments: every topic assigned to researcher has a due date and status

agent_health.name: wiki-librarian
agent_health.description:
Information architect and publisher for the wiki team. Owns wiki structure, taxonomy, cross-references, and the machine-readable index (wiki-index.md). Publishes approved articles from staging to artifacts, maintains navigation, handles archival. Nothing goes live without the librarian.

Success metrics: wiki-index.md updated within same session as every publish or archive operation; SITEMAP.md reflects current wiki structure — zero orphaned or missing entries; 9-step publishing workflow completed fully per article: no skipped validation steps; Cross-references validated: zero broken wikilinks in published articles (auditable via build-wiki-index.py); SharePoint .docx published to correct Artifacts/{category}/ path for every FINAL article

agent_health.name: wiki-researcher
agent_health.description:
Research agent for the wiki team. Gathers source material from body system organs, DuckDB analytics, Slack, Outlook email, internal Amazon resources, ARCC, and web sources. Produces structured research briefs for the wiki-writer. Never writes wiki pages directly.

Success metrics: Every research brief cites ≥3 sources with [source: type, date, confidence] format; Primary sources preferred: ≥50% of citations from body system organs or DuckDB (not web fallback); Research brief delivered within single invocation — no back-and-forth required; Contradictions between sources explicitly flagged with confidence assessment; ABPS AI research subtask completed and brief posted as pinned comment when applicable

agent_health.name: wiki-writer
agent_health.description:
Narrative agent for the wiki team. Transforms research briefs into polished wiki articles optimized for both human readers and agent consumption. Loads all 3 Richard writing style guides before producing any output. Writes to staging files or Asana html_notes. Never publishes directly.

Success metrics: Critic score ≥8/10 on first submission (target: >60% of articles pass without revision); 100% adherence to Amazon narrative standard: prose-driven, 18-20 word sentence average, no bullet abuse; Draft word count within target range per article type (guide: 500-1500, reference: 300-800); All 3 style guides loaded and applied per invocation (richard-writing-style, richard-style-docs, richard-style-amazon); < 2 revision cycles per article from draft to PUBLISH verdict (tracked via draft-log.md timestamps)
